Dashboard Archive: 2026-04-02 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data reveals two extreme momentum outliers at the top of the rankings. PL dominates with a score of 97.1 and 1-year return of 934.4%, though its momentum signal of 2.4 flags it as "overextended"—a warning sign that such explosive gains may face pullback risk. FSLY follows closely with a more moderate but still exceptional 424.31% annual return and a negative bad_spy_score of -0.1877, indicating it actually outperforms when broader markets struggle, which is a genuine strength metric. Both tickers pass all SMA gates (trading above their 30/50/100/200-day averages) and maintain consistent return ratios across timeframes, suggesting sustained upward momentum rather than single-timeframe spikes. The key distinction is that PL's 11.69 ratio between 1-year and 3-month returns suggests acceleration into a potentially unsustainable zone, while FSLY's more measured 1.84 ratio indicates steadier, less volatile appreciation. Together, these rankings highlight how the dashboard rewards both extreme momentum (PL) and resilient outperformance (FSLY), but marks the former with caution flags.
